title: Keep platform core as contracts and move backend bindings to adapters
context: Platform contracts need to support independent evolution of inventory, delivery, supplier, payment, marketing, analytics, and order access capabilities.
decision: The platform core must not import Medusa framework packages or built-in modules. Built-in registration, service tokens, and capability scopes live in platform-adapters.
consequences: Replacing a module should not require editing platform core exports.
Capability handlers receive restricted backend scopes rather than the raw container.
Platform boundary drift is treated as a machine-checkable failure.
